articles.headline: World Cup final squads ranked: Of all 48 national teams, who can win this summer?
articles.description: We have the final 26-player squads that can win the FIFA World Cup, so let's go through all 48 teams and rank where they stand.

images.caption: Mauricio Pochettino speaks after the USMNT's 3-2 win over Senegal ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup trophy New York [1296x729]
images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Nigeria files World Cup complaint to FIFA over Congo DR
articles.description: Nigeria are hoping to revive their hopes of appearing at the 2026 World Cup with complaint to FIFA over the use of at least nine ineligible players by Congo DR.

images.name: DR Congo vs. Nigeria [1296x729]
images.caption: Nigeria were beaten on penalties by DR Congo in their 2026 World Cup playoff.
images.alt: Alex Iwobi of Nigeria in action against Aaron Wan-Bissaka of DR Congo during the FIFA World Cup 26 CAF qualifiers play-offs final match between Nigeria and DR Congo, in Rabat, Morocco, on 16 November 2025

articles.headline: Can Tunisia cause an upset in Group F?
articles.description: Ed Dove discusses Tunisia's chances of progression from Group F as they face tough opponents Japan, The Netherlands and a UEFA play off winner.

keyEvents.text: Goal! Gambia 0, Gabon 1. Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang (Gabon) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Denis Bouanga.
Goal! Gambia 1, Gabon 1. Yankuba Minteh (Gambia) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Baboucarr Gaye.
Mario Lemina (Gabon) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Gambia 1, Gabon 2. Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang (Gabon) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Loyce Mbaba.
Joseph Ceesay (Gambia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Gambia 2, Gabon 2. Adama Sidibeh (Gambia) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Yankuba Minteh.
Substitution, Gabon. Aaron Appindangoyé replaces Noha Lemina.
Substitution, Gabon. Teddy Averlant replaces Mick Onfia.
Goal! Gambia 3, Gabon 2. Adama Sidibeh (Gambia) header from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal.
Substitution, Gambia. Muhammed Sanneh replaces Joseph Ceesay.
Goal! Gambia 3, Gabon 3. Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang (Gabon) header from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Guélor Kanga.
Substitution, Gambia. Abubakr Barry replaces Ebou Adams.
Guélor Kanga (Gabon) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Gabon. André Poko replaces Mario Lemina.
Substitution, Gabon. Edlin Matouti replaces Denis Bouanga.
Goal! Gambia 3, Gabon 4. Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang (Gabon) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Edlin Matouti.
Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang (Gabon) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Muhammed Sanneh (Gambia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Gambia. Alieu Fadera replaces Abubacarr Kinteh.
Substitution, Gambia. Ali Sowe replaces Adama Sidibeh.
Second yellow card to Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang (Gabon) for a bad foul.
Substitution, Gabon. Yannis M'Bemba replaces Teddy Averlant.
Aaron Appindangoyé (Gabon) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
